title: Chapter Fifty-Nine: The Blood Hunt
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

Five days south of Pingyin County, and Yeon-shin stood before the largest pleasure house in Xuzhou.

Multicolored lanterns hung above and below the main gate, brilliant against the deepening sky. The city was large and splendid -- the kind of place where martial families flourished under the Namgung family's distant influence. Orthodox territory. South Zhili.

He had spent those five days showing his Desolate token to magistrates in every government office he passed. Asking about suspicious characters. Blood Flame Sect members roaming the cities, they said. Fearing nothing. Escaping soldiers with swift body techniques or threatening them with energy waves. Growing bolder by the day.

The days Mi-ryeo had estimated were shrinking.

*If not the ones said to be here.*

He pushed the wooden doors wide.

*Clunk.*

Orange lamplight spilled out. The first floor was a socializing hall, packed with martial artists -- easily fifty, their complexions flushed with drink or bright with the glow of candlelight. All sorts of figures, men and women both, some carrying weapons openly.

Many gazes swept over him. Energy in those looks. These were not merchants.

"Who is that child?"

"Hwang? Is he imitating Desolate Sect?"

"Boy! There are other places for reckless behavior!"

"Even blue-ranked!"

Laughter. Boisterous, contemptuous laughter from people who had never seen a Desolate warrior walk among them.

Yeon-shin took a step forward.

The small footstep carried a strange power. Something that reached beyond hearing into energy sense. He was releasing his momentum -- the full weight of a blue-ranked master's presence, unfurling like a banner.

Silence spread with each step. Those who had been making comments closed their mouths. Massive questions and silent surprise filled the space where laughter had been.

His vision caught those standing up from corners. Black-haired, all of them, but he felt it instantly. Day by day, his achievement with the Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm was rising. He had become extremely sensitive to sinister true qi.

"A branch is here," he said.

One Blood Swordmaster and five Blood Flame Sect members. He knew from the size of their energy waves.

"Like fire ants, branches must be everywhere. Not just you."

The young man who stood smiling, seemingly confident in his martial might, was the Blood Swordmaster. A noble without a single scar on his face.

"So you are that one? Diligently offering tribute brings fortune! I will take one step toward the Apostle realm!"

Yeon-shin ignored him.

"You must want to kill the owner of the Demon-Destroying Azure Unyielding Palm."

He operated verses mixed with dharma. Refreshing energy drove away the pleasure house's alcohol smell. From his right hand, lowered at an angle, precise energy waves began to spread. Subtle blue radiance surrounded his palm -- purely blue, unmixed with the lantern light around him.

The Blood Swordmaster charged. Chilling momentum erupted from his body. His sword draw rose like blood bursting, fierce as a storm.

Yeon-shin struck down with the Azure Unyielding Palm. The moves were clear.

A smooth smile formed on the Blood Swordmaster's lips. He seemed intent on cutting the hand aiming for his head.

*Kung!*

Not a collision.

A shattering. The gale of energy spewed from the man's sword was crushed outright. Half a technique to break the energy wave completely, shattering the sword with it. Yeon-shin rode the move down -- riding the straight line of force to its inevitable conclusion.

His blade hand completed the technique at the fellow's skull.

*Pheok.*

He felt it with his palm. Deep. The Blood Swordmaster's crown sank inward with fragments of blue energy force. The man fell, brushing past Yeon-shin's shoulder. His smiling face remained unchanged even in death.

Four more Blood Flame Sect members behind him. Their faces could not be described. They stood frozen, unable to charge.

Their branch leader had died in one technique.

Even the martial artists who had jumped up sensing the Blood Flame Sect's energy wave kept silent.

"Relay this," Yeon-shin said.

He dusted his palm once. His blue sleeve fluttered.

"Radiant Demon Wing's Lightning Flash is here."

---
